SEARCH
Package edu.bu.pas.risk.agent

Class FSMAgent

java.lang.Object
edu.bu.pas.risk.agent.FSMAgent
All Implemented Interfaces:
IAgent, RiskListener, Cloneable
Direct Known Subclasses:
NeuralQAgent
public abstract class FSMAgent extends Object implements IAgent

Represents a class that moves through a finite state machine when deciding what Actions to make during its turn. See IAgent for a description of what Actions can be played when (e.g. what FSMAgent.MovePhase) during a turn.

The states of this state machine are stored in the FSMAgent.MovePhase enum and behave according to the following transition function:

  1. At the beginning of your turn you are allowed to redeem cards if you have enough to redeem. In this phase I will generate all possible trade-ins of cards in your inventory and ask your model to rank that trade-in. Your model will either pick a trade in (at which point that trade-in will occur and this process repeats) or it will select NoAction to transition to the next phase of the turn.
  2. Once transitioned from the redeeming phase (or if you couldn't redeem cards in the first place) its time to use your armies to attack an adjacent territory. I will generate all possible options and ask your model to rank each choice. If the best choice is an AttackAction, that action will occur and this process repeated. If the best choice is a NoAction the agent will transition to the next phase of the turn. NOTE that if you eliminate an opponent you get the cards in their possession. If the amount of cards in your hand exceeds 6 then instead of ranking AttackActions and NoAction you will rank RedeemCardsActions until the number of cards in your hand drops to 4 or less. Then we will resume the process of ranking AttackActions and NoAction.
  3. Once transitioned to this state your agent will have to choose which territory you control to fortify or to skip fortification. I will generate all possible FortifyActions as well as a NoAction and ask your model to rank them. Whichever is the best will occur and your turn will end.
Author:
Andrew Wood

  • Method Details

    • agentId

      public int agentId()
      Returns the agent's unique id and turn order, e.g. 0 for the player who goes first, 1 for the player who goes 2nd, etc.
      Specified by:
      agentId in interface IAgent
      Returns:
      The unique, sorted, agent id.

    • getMovePhase

      public final FSMAgent.MovePhase getMovePhase()

    • getAction

      public final Action getAction(GameView game, int actionCounter, boolean canRedeemCards)
      Returns this agent's choice of an action at the given game state.

      The Risk code will call this method many times, until you return a FortifyAction or a NoAction to end your turn. Every time this method is called, we increment the actionCounter by 1. This means at the start of your turn, it will always be 0.

      The order of actions is as follows:

      1. Any number, including 0 RedeemCardsActions
      2. Any number, including 0 AttackActions
      3. If eliminating an agent gives you 6 or more territory cards, then you must immediately trade in enough sets to reduce your hand to 4 or fewer cards. But once your hand is reduced to 4, 3, or 2 cards, you must stop trading.
      4. Exactly 1 of FortifyAction or NoAction (to skip fortify)
      Specified by:
      getAction in interface IAgent
      Parameters:
      game - The game state.
      actionCounter - How many actions you have taken so far this turn. Starts at 0 always.
      canRedeemCards - If you are allowed to send in a RedeemCardsAction. This is true when you have not started your attack phase, and have enough cards to trade in. This is also true when you have eliminated an opponent and now have 6 or more territory cards.
      Returns:
      The action you choose to do at this point in the game.

    • onTurnStart

      public void onTurnStart(GameView game, int agentIdx)
      Description copied from interface: RiskListener
      Called at the start of each agent's turn.
      Specified by:
      onTurnStart in interface RiskListener
      Parameters:
      game - The game state BEFORE an agent's turn starts.
      agentIdx - Whose turn it is.

    • onTurnEnd

      public void onTurnEnd(GameView game, int agentIdx)
      Description copied from interface: RiskListener
      Called when a turn ends.
      Specified by:
      onTurnEnd in interface RiskListener
      Parameters:
      game - The game state AFTER the turn has ended and all actions have resolved.
      agentIdx - Whose turn it was.

    • chooseRedeemAction

      public abstract Action chooseRedeemAction(GameView game, int actionCounter, boolean canRedeemCards)
      A method to get the Action when you are in the redeeming cards phase of your turn.
      Parameters:
      game - The game state.
      actionCounter - How many actions you have taken so far this turn. Starts at 0 always.
      canRedeemCards - If you are allowed to send in a RedeemCardsAction. This is true when you have not started your attack phase, and have enough cards to trade in. This is also true when you have eliminated an opponent and now have 6 or more territory cards.
      Returns:
      The action you choose to do at this point in the game.

    • chooseAttackActionRedeemIfForced

      public abstract Action chooseAttackActionRedeemIfForced(GameView game, int actionCounter, boolean canRedeemCards)
      A method to get the Action when you are in the attack (and maybe redeem) phase of your turn
      Parameters:
      game - The game state.
      actionCounter - How many actions you have taken so far this turn. Starts at 0 always.
      canRedeemCards - If you are allowed to send in a RedeemCardsAction. This is true when you have not started your attack phase, and have enough cards to trade in. This is also true when you have eliminated an opponent and now have 6 or more territory cards.
      Returns:
      The action you choose to do at this point in the game.

    • chooseFortifySkipAction

      public abstract Action chooseFortifySkipAction(GameView game, int actionCounter, boolean canRedeemCards)
      A method to get the Action when you are in the fortify/skip fortify phase of your turn.
      Parameters:
      game - The game state.
      actionCounter - How many actions you have taken so far this turn. Starts at 0 always.
      canRedeemCards - If you are allowed to send in a RedeemCardsAction. This is true when you have not started your attack phase, and have enough cards to trade in. This is also true when you have eliminated an opponent and now have 6 or more territory cards.
      Returns:
      The action you choose to do at this point in the game.
